name: Mehran Keshe
description: Mehran Tavakoli Keshe is an Iranian nuclear engineer who founded the Keshe Foundation. He claims to have developed revolutionary technologies based on 'plasma reactors' capable of producing free energy, anti-gravity, healing, and manipulating matter at the atomic level. These technologies, according to Keshe, are based on his understanding of 'Magnetical and Gravitational fields' (Magravs) and their interaction. Keshe asserts his technologies can solve global problems like energy scarcity, pollution, and disease. He has attempted to distribute his technology through open-source initiatives, but these efforts have been met with skepticism and criticism. Mainstream science views Keshe's claims as pseudoscience, lacking credible scientific evidence and failing to adhere to established scientific principles. His theories often contradict accepted physics and his demonstrations haven't been independently verified. Critics argue his claims are unsubstantiated and that his technologies are ineffective, potentially harmful, and exploit people's desire for solutions to complex problems. The Keshe Foundation has faced scrutiny for its business practices and the sale of unproven products. While Keshe has gained a following through online communities and workshops, his work is largely dismissed by the scientific community due to the lack of empirical validation and the incompatibility of his claims with established scientific knowledge.
subtitle: Plasma technology and free energy claims

claims.evidence: Keshe's demonstrations and testimonials from followers.
claims.criticism: No independent verification, violates laws of thermodynamics, lacks peer-reviewed publications.

claims.evidence: Anecdotal reports from users of Keshe's products.
claims.criticism: No clinical trials, placebo effect likely explanation, potentially dangerous to rely on unproven treatments.

claims.evidence: Demonstrations involving levitation of small objects.
claims.criticism: Demonstrations not reproducible under controlled conditions, no theoretical basis in physics, likely utilizes conventional methods.

claims.claim: Plasma reactors can purify water and clean the environment.
claims.evidence: Claims made by the Keshe Foundation based on their experiments.
claims.criticism: Lack of peer-reviewed scientific studies to support these claims, potential for harmful byproducts in unverified devices.

keyFigures.name: Mehran Tavakoli Keshe
keyFigures.role: Founder of the Keshe Foundation, proponent of plasma technology

keyFigures.name: Dr. David Ashworth
keyFigures.role: Physicist and Critic who has debunked many of Keshe's claims

mythologyConnections: Alchemical transmutation (loosely connected through the idea of transforming matter)
Gnostic concepts of creation and energy (vaguely similar in terms of unseen forces shaping reality)

timeline.event: Keshe announces the release of 'free energy' technology.
Keshe calls on world leaders to adopt his technology.
Keshe Foundation continues to promote and sell plasma-based products.

sources.author: Keshe, M.T.
sources.work: The Universal Order of Creation of Matters
sources.title: Keshe, M.T.
